channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 1,719, more than 5,000 injured
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: What aftermath for Venezuela? Earthquake relief hampered by political crisis
item.description: Kudos to Venezuela's first responders and all the brave citizens still pulling survivors from under the rubble of the 774 buildings that collapsed in last Wednesday’s double earthquakes. Is the sudden pancaking of so many structures - structures built during the boom years of high oil prices, a tipping point for an under-sanctions nation dogged by decades of eroding services and infrastructure? The interim president of a regime in survival mode was copiously booed Saturday in Caracas as many accuse the successor of Hugo Chavez and Nicolas Maduro of putting the security apparatus before disaster relief.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Pragmatic diplomacy: Dawud Al Ansari says 'Oman is committed to de-escalation & peace in the region'
item.description: As French President Emmanuel Macron meets Sultan Haitham bin Tariq of Oman amid efforts to de-escalate tensions in the Middle East, François Picard welcomes Dawud Al Ansari, a geopolitics and energy analyst. As diplomacy in the Gulf is once again being tested by renewed military tensions, Al Ansari offers a nuanced assessment from an Omani perspective, arguing that Oman is playing an increasingly valuable strategic role.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Aftershock hits Caracas as rescue efforts enter critical hours in Venezuela
item.description: Residents of ​Caracas woke ‌up on Monday to an aftershock that rocked their ⁠houses, while rescue teams continued their fourth day of round-the-clock work in the areas affected by last week's powerful ‌earthquakes in Venezuela. France 24's Noris Soto has the latest from the Venezuelan capital.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French govt under fire over heatwave response
item.description: France's government has come under fire from parties on both the left and right over what they say was a lack of preparedness for the recent record heatwave that swept across Europe, with the country recording around 1,000 excess deaths. France 24's Marc Perelman takes a closer look.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill dozens of civilians in Afghanistan, officials say
item.description: Pakistani forces' ground operations and strikes killed at least 36 civilians in Afghanistan overnight and wounded more than 160 others, Afghan officials said Monday, as tensions between the neighbours escalated. One Afghan official said the attacks would be met with retaliation. Pakistan said the operations were launched in response to militant attacks across Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US Supreme Court expands Trump's power to fire officials, but protects Fed
item.description: The US Supreme Court on Monday boosted presidential power, upholding President Donald Trump’s firings of the heads of independent federal agencies with one important exception: the Federal Reserve.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: South Africa: Thousands of foreigners flee ahead of anti-immigration 'deadline'
item.description: Anti-#immigration groups in #SouthAfrica have set a 'deadline': by June 30 all undocumented immigrants must have left the country, or groups will 'shut the country down'. The weeks up to the 'deadline' have been marred by xenophobic violence, from attacks on foreign-owned businesses to #immigrants killed during #protests. Demonstrators accuse them, without evidence, of stealing jobs - despite them representing about 4% of the population -, collapsing public services and #crime.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Brazil vs Japan live: Casemiro levels after Sano put the Samurai Blue ahead
item.description: Brazil's quest for a sixth World Cup title enters the knockouts on Monday as they face Japan in Houston for a place in the last 16. Carlo Ancelotti’s men are favourites but mindful of the threat posed by tournament dark horses Japan, having lost 3-2 when the teams last met in a friendly. Follow our live, minute-by-minute match commentary.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: Senegal president Faye to call referendum on constitutional reform limiting his powers
item.description: Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye will call a referendum on a proposed constitutional amendment that would strengthen the power of parliament and the prime minister, the country's justice minister said Monday. The country's National Assembly overwhelmingly backed the reform in a vote earlier in the day that saw roughly 50 opposition protesters attempt to storm the building.
item.source: © Abdel Majd Bziouat, AFP / Présidence de la République du Sénégal
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Jazz à Vienne celebrates late Miles Davis for 45th edition
item.description:
In this edition of arts24, Marjorie Hache is in the town of Vienne in southeastern France, where the 45th edition of "Jazz à Vienne" is taking place. Headliners include the likes of Jon Batiste, Marcus Miller, Buena Vista Social Club and Jeff Mills. The festival is also shining a spotlight on late jazz legend Miles Davis, 100 years since his birth. The American musician played at the festival four times during his life and left a lasting mark on the town.
Marjorie also shares one of her festival highlights: Cameroonian band Akutuk's concert in a swimming pool in an ode to their ancestral links with water. She also chats with New Orleans trumpeter James Andrews, who is transforming the streets of Vienne into a giant parade.
item.dc:creator: Marjorie HACHE

item.title: French hospitals 'better prepared': 'Important lessons learned from deadly 2003 heatwave'
item.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Antoine Flahault, Epidemiologist, Researcher, and Professor at Université Paris Cité - Hospital Bichat. France's latest heatwave has once again exposed healthcare systems were largely designed for another era. While the immediate focus often falls on soaring temperatures and overcrowded emergency departments, Dr. Flahault argues that the deeper issue is institutional adaptation. He suggests that France has learned important lessons since the deadly 2003 heatwave, with hospitals adopting better clinical practices and emergency planning. Yet as heatwaves become more frequent and intense, protecting vulnerable populations will increasingly require structural investment, particularly widespread access to air conditioning in hospitals, care homes, schools and, for those most at risk, private homes.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: EU refuses to take side for or against air conditioning amid record-breaking heatwave
item.description: As Europe’s deadly heatwave moves east, Brussels said Monday it would not pick a side in the heated political debate for or against air conditioning.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Trump says Iran talks to be held in Qatar, but Tehran denies it
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: All warring parties in Mali committed 'grave abuses' against civilians, report says
item.description: Al-Qaeda-linked jihadists, the Malian army and its Russian allies have all committed "grave abuses" against civilians during and after the April attacks that rocked the West African country, Human Rights Watch said in a report on Monday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Why Oman has become a key diplomatic player in Mideast war
item.description: Iran said Monday it had held its first meeting with Oman on managing the Strait of Hormuz since it signed a preliminary deal to end the Middle East war with the United States. It comes as French President Emmanuel Macron is set to host the Sultan of Oman at the Elysée Palace on Monday, with talks also lilely to focus on Hormuz. France 244s James Andre takes a look at how Oman ended up playing a key diplomatic role in the conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Afghanistan: at least 36 killed in deadly Pakistani strikes
item.description: At least 36 people were killed across three provinces of eastern #Afghanistan in Pakistani strikes. #Islamabad says it was targeting a group responsible for an attack the day before.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: What happens when lightning strikes the Eiffel Tower?
item.description: The heatwave that's seen much of northern Europe melting in the past week is moving eastwards, with Germany and Hungary reaching or exceeding 40°C in the past 24 hours.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Funeral homes overwhelmed as France counts human cost of heatwave
item.description: As the deadly heatwave moves eastward to other parts of Europe, France is counting the human cost the high temperatures have left behind. French health authorities have reported around 1,000 excess deaths during the heatwave. Hospitals are overflowing with patients and funeral homes are struggling to keep up.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Syria slams new Israeli incursion in southern Daraa province
item.description: The Syrian foreign ministry on Monday condemned Israeli incursions and bombardment in the country's south a day after Israeli forces advanced into the village of Abidin, where angry residents tried to block the road with stones to stop the patrol. The Israeli military has conducted at least 70 incursions into southern Syria this month alone, according to the local NGO Sijil.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Americans' pride in US history and democracy dropped steeply over past decade, poll finds
item.description: Americans’ pride in their country's history or the way its democracy works has dropped noticeably over the past decade, according to a new poll from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: DR Congo conflict: Rwandan FM 'disappointed by increasingly biased US mediation'
item.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Rwandan Foreign Minister Olivier Nduhungirehe said his country is "disappointed by the increasingly biased US mediation" in the conflict with DR Congo. He also accused the US of sanctioning Kigali alone while Kinshasa fails to honour the 2025 Washington Accords between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Marc PERELMAN

item.title: Thousands of Vespas swarm Rome to mark scooter’s 80th anniversary
item.description: The Vespa was celebrating its 80th birthday on Saturday with thousands of riders parading through the historic centre of Rome to celebrate the legendary scooter, which has become an icon of the Italian way of life.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
